pubmed_links: [Seiffert et al. (2022) FGF12 variants modulate NaV1.2 and NaV1.6 in developmental epileptic encephalopathy and autism spectrum disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36029553/); [Willemsen et al. (2020) Epilepsy phenotype in individuals with chromosomal duplication encompassing FGF12](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32524056/); [Guella et al. (2016) De novo FGF12 mutation in 2 patients with neonatal-onset epilepsy](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27872899/)
summary: Variants in FGF12, encoding fibroblast homologous factor 1 (FHF1) that modulates voltage-gated sodium channels NaV1.2 and NaV1.6, cause developmental and epileptic encephalopathy-47 (DEE47) and have been associated with autism spectrum disorder; gain-of-function point mutations and chromosomal duplications of FGF12 produce early-onset refractory seizures with phenytoin-responsiveness, reflecting the gene's critical role in neuronal excitability. (emerging evidence)
